source_metadata.title_raw: Blaise J. Jackson - A-18-088 - May 16, 2018 - San Diego

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 16, 2018

Blaise J. Jackson
Special Counsel to Palomar Health
Law Offices Scott & Jackson
16935 West Bernardo Drive, Suite 170
San Diego, CA 92127

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-18-088

Dear Mr. Jackson:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the Palomar Health District ("PH") regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

QUESTION

Under the Act's conflict of interest provisions, does PH Board Member, G. Douglas Moir, M.D. have a potentially disqualifying interest in Graybill Medical Group ("GMG") in light of the fact that he previously contracted with GMB for billing and collections work?

CONCLUSION

No. Dr. Moir does not have a potentially disqualifying interest in GMG. Barring additional facts, Dr. Moir is not prohibited from taking part in decisions implicating GMG or GMG's competitors.

___________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-088
Page No. 2

FACTS AS PROVIDED BY REQUESTOR

Your office serves as special Board Counsel for PH, a California Healthcare District. You
seek advice on behalf of PH with respect to an assertion of a possible disqualifying conflict of
interest raised with respect to a member of the PH Board, G. Douglas Moir, M.D.

PH is organized under the provisions of the California Health and Safety Code Section
32000 et seq., and is the owner/operator of Palomar Hospital Escondido, Palomar Hospital
Downtown Campus and Palomar Hospital Poway (f/k/a Pomerado Hospital). Geographically, PH is
the largest healthcare district in San Diego County, serving over 600,000 district residents.

Dr. Moir was a long-standing physician in Escondido, specializing in cardiology prior to
winning a seat on the PH Board in November 2016. He closed his practice in Escondido in 2017 and
has retired. While an active physician, about thirty percent of Dr. Moir's patients saw him through
"managed care" coverage configuration through affiliation with Sharp Community Medical Group
("SCMG"). For managed care patients in Northern San Diego County, SCMG contracted with two
different physician groups - Inland Physicians North ("IP North") and GMG. Prior to 2014, Dr.
Moir attended his managed care patients as one of the approved panel physicians aligned with IP
North.

In 2014, Dr. Moir contracted with GMG for billing and collections work. The specific
arrangement provided compensation to GMG for its services for a small percentage of the
collections revenue. At this same time, Dr. Moir also switched panel affiliations from IP North to
GMG for his managed care clientele. Dr. Moir was not and has never been a physician member of
GMG, nor did he become a partner, equity partner or part owner of GMG. The business relationship
was limited to contracting with GMG for billing and collections. In a telephone conversation, you
stated that Dr. Moir was on a list of panel doctors, and that it would be up to each individual patient
to select a physician from that list. You also stated that GMG provided only billing and collection
services to Dr. Moir, and that payment for services was proved by an insurance company, United
Health, through SCMG. It is your understanding that the switch from IP North to GMG actually
resulted in an approximate ten percent decrease in Dr. Moir's managed care patient load, which
remained true until the time of his retirement last year. Upon retirement, Dr. Moir's managed care
patients migrated to other physicians on the approved panel and Dr. Moir referred his conventional
insurance/fee-for-service patients to other physicians throughout Northern San Diego County.

In 2010, PH helped organize a medical foundation as a nonprofit public benefit corporation
to provide and carry out charitable healthcare functions in multi-specialty medical clinics in the
communities served by the District. The organization also established a new Independent
Physicians' Association in North County. Originally named Palomar Physicians' Network, the
entity's name changed in late 2010 to Arch Health Partners ("Arch"). Initially consisting of primary
care physicians, Arch has since branched into other specialty areas, including orthopedic surgery.
For most of its history, Arch has received significant financial support from PH through a District-
provided line of credit in order to further its mission of providing community primary care services.
For most of its history, Arch has been a competitor practice organization to GMG. For the past
several years, representatives from GMG have periodically appeared at PH Board meetings,

File No. A-18-088
Page No. 3

utilizing the "public comment" item on the agenda to speak critically to the PH Board about its
expansion of the Arch line of credit.

In mid-2017, the PH Board directed management to engage the services of an outside
evaluation firm to assess Arch's business/practice operations and report to PH on the relative
value to the PH community of the ongoing support, which PH had been providing. Arch,
through its counsel, has expressed concerns about Dr. Moir having any involvement in that
process, citing what it believed to be Dr. Moir's disqualifying financial interest in GMG
and requesting that Dr. Moir be insulated from any involvement in the Navigant assessment
of Arch's operations and the efficacy of PH support of Arch.

ANALYSIS

Conflict of Interest Law

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest.
(Section 87103.) Section 87103 defines the interests from which a conflict of interest may arise.
These interests are:

1. Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment²
worth $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(a).)

2. Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth
$2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b).)

3. Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in
the regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the
public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section
87103(c).)

4. Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).)

5. Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
[$470] or more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).)

6. Personal finances, meaning the financial effect of a governmental decision on the
personal finances of a public official or his or her immediate family.

You have stated that Dr. Moir has never been a partner, equity partner, or part owner of
GMG. Dr. Moir's business relationship with GMG was limited to contracting for billing and

² "Indirect investment" is defined to include any investment or interest owned by the official's spouse. (Section
87103.)

File No. A-18-088
Page No. 4

collections. Dr. Moir does not have and ownership interest in, nor does he hold an employment or
management position in GBG. It also does not appear that Dr. Moir has received income from
GBG. Income for his services as a doctor is received from United Health, through SCMG.
Moreover, to the extent that the patient selected Dr. Moir for the services, the income is generally
attributed to the patient. (See Hurtado Advice Letter, No. A-17-100.) As such, Dr. Moir does not
have a potentially disqualifying interest in GMG under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act.
Accordingly, he is not prohibited from taking part in decisions implicating GMG or GMG's
competitors.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Brian G. Lau
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Zachary W. Norton
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

ZWN:jgl
